advisory_pre_existing.finding: Line contains Chinese text in a user-visible BLOCKED error message: 'BLOCKED: 必须使用正常的UI流程创建session，永远不允许使用代码创建session！'. Line is unchanged baseline text — not introduced by this cycle's diff.
advisory_pre_existing.recommendation: Translate the BLOCKED message to English. Per Standard 6 exemption clause (task-id 20260509-153155), verbatim user-binding quotes belong in docs/dev/ticket-*.md only. Create a ticket artifact (e.g. docs/dev/ticket-20260405-135821.md) preserving the original Chinese text, then replace the inline Chinese with an English paraphrase plus task-id attribution.

advisory_pre_existing.finding: Line contains Chinese text in the module-level docstring: 'prompt `全部commit push`'. Line is unchanged baseline text — not introduced by this cycle's diff.
advisory_pre_existing.recommendation: Translate the docstring reference to English. Create a ticket artifact (e.g. docs/dev/ticket-20260421-174500.md) preserving the verbatim Chinese prompt text, then replace the inline Chinese with an English paraphrase ('prompt: commit and push all') plus task-id attribution.

standards_passed: no-inline-code (N/A: hook scripts are not .md command/agent files)

no-hardcoded-values (hardcoded /tmp paths, /root/happy* paths, and Life AI domain constants exist but are intentional hook-protocol or safety-policy constants keyed by runtime input; this diff introduces no new configurable hardcoded values)
use-source-venv (no bare python3 calls; all python invocations use $PYTHON_BIN derived from venv)
integer-step-numbering (N/A: integer step numbering applies to command/agent .md files, not hook scripts)
meaningful-naming (pretool-bash-safety.sh and pretool-git-privilege-guard.py are descriptive names)
concise-documentation (N/A: hook scripts are not .md command/agent files)
parameterized-scripts (both scripts accept input via stdin JSON per hook protocol; no new hardcoded configurable values introduced in this diff)
dangling-references (no script cross-references to non-existent files found)

diff_summary.pretool-bash-safety.sh: 1 line added: check_and_consume_allowlist call inserted before BLOCKED systemctl message (~line 805). No new English violations in changed lines.
diff_summary.pretool-git-privilege-guard.py: _evaluate_push signature refactored from (command, sid) to (command, data) with sid derived internally; _check_git_allowlist call added for push path. No new English violations in changed lines.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed both violations are real Standard 6 findings but pre-existing baseline text outside this cycle's changed-line range. Also corrected wording for violation 2 (module-level docstring, not comment), flagged that the ticket-cite recommendations reference non-existent ticket artifacts, noted that Standard 2 rationale was overstated and should acknowledge that hardcoded values exist as intentional policy constants, and noted Standard 4 should be framed as N/A for hook scripts rather than 'no patterns found'.

findings.issue: Violations 1 and 2 are real Standard 6 findings, but they are pre-existing baseline text outside this diff's changed-line scope (changed lines are pretool-bash-safety.sh:805 and pretool-git-privilege-guard.py:571-572,583-584,666; findings are at :903 and :11).
findings.proposed_fix: Move both from blocking violations[] to advisory_pre_existing[], noting 'Real English-only baseline violation, but not introduced by this cycle; advisory only for changed-files cleanliness.'
findings.rationale: Git diff confirms the Chinese text at both locations is unchanged baseline. Per AC-2.6(b) line-level rule, a finding is provably NEW only when its line falls within the diff's changed-line range. Neither line 903 nor line 11 appears in the diff hunks. Moved to advisory_pre_existing.

findings.issue: Violation 2 wording says 'docstring comment'; it is a module-level docstring, not a comment.
findings.proposed_fix: Change finding to: 'Line contains Chinese text in the module-level docstring: prompt `全部commit push`.'

findings.issue: Recommendations cite ticket artifacts that may not yet exist (docs/dev/ticket-20260421-174500.md, docs/dev/ticket-20260405-135821.md).
findings.proposed_fix: Create/cite ticket artifacts before referencing them.
findings.rationale: Recommendations updated to specify the ticket artifact creation step explicitly rather than assuming they exist.

findings.issue: Standard 2 clean rationale was overstated: files contain hardcoded /tmp, /root/happy*, and Life AI domain constants; they are not 'env-var-parameterized paths throughout'.
findings.proposed_fix: Reword to: 'Hardcoded paths/domains exist, but they are intentional hook protocol or safety-policy constants keyed by runtime input; this diff introduces no new configurable hardcoded values.'
findings.rationale: standards_passed entry for no-hardcoded-values updated with accurate rationale.

findings.issue: Standard 4 clean rationale said 'no step numbering patterns found' instead of explaining it is N/A for hook scripts.
findings.proposed_fix: Reword to: 'Standard 4 N/A — integer step numbering applies to command/agent .md files, not hook scripts.'
findings.rationale: standards_passed entry for integer-step-numbering updated with N/A framing.

findings.issue: No missed critical style violations in the actual changed hunks.
findings.rationale: Codex confirmed no missed violations in the diff content.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All blocker and major findings applied: both English-only findings moved to advisory_pre_existing with diff_overlap: false; violation 2 wording corrected to 'module-level docstring'; Standard 2 and Standard 4 rationale updated in standards_passed; ticket artifact creation steps added to recommendations.
